```text
3 · Advanced · Powerlifting · 4d/wk · meet in 10 weeks · S/B/D given → peaking
================================================================================================
WHY: Built parametrically for a advanced lifter (~6yr training age) chasing maximal strength. Peaking backward from your meet in 10 weeks: Strength 7wk -> Peak 3wk.

  Weekly per-muscle set plan (freq/wk · week-1 sets → peak-week sets · MEV–MRV landmark):
     abs          1×/wk   9→17     sets   (MEV 6 · MRV 25)
     back         3×/wk   14→22    sets   (MEV 10 · MRV 25)
     biceps       1×/wk   9→17     sets   (MEV 8 · MRV 20)
     chest        2×/wk   14→22    sets   (MEV 10 · MRV 22)

  RULE TRACE (why these numbers):
     • Experience "advanced" (~6yr training age): base 17 sets/muscle/wk, +2/wk ramp, ≤9 sets/muscle/session, periodization="block".
     • Goal "strength_powerlifting": volume ×0.85, RIR band 1–3; rep zones primary 1–5 @ 87%1RM, accessory 8–12 @ 70%1RM.
     • RIR floors (never trained closer than): compound 1, isolation 1. Knob shift +0. Novice self-report calibration +0.
     • Deload cadence: every 5 weeks. Progression load ×1.
     • Split for 4 day(s): Squat Focus · Bench Focus · Deadlift Focus · Upper Accessory.
     • Per-session cap: quads weekly target trimmed to 9×1 sessions to stay ≤9 sets/session (junk-volume ceiling, RESEARCH.md §4.2).
     • Per-session cap: shoulders weekly target trimmed to 9×1 sessions to stay ≤9 sets/session (junk-volume ceiling, RESEARCH.md §4.2).
     • Per-session cap: traps weekly target trimmed to 9×1 sessions to stay ≤9 sets/session (junk-volume ceiling, RESEARCH.md §4.2).


================================================================================================
4 · Time-crunched · Fat-loss / general · 3d/wk · 30 min
================================================================================================
WHY: Built parametrically for a novice lifter (~2yr training age) chasing general fitness / recomp. Volume ramps from ~MEV toward MRV over 5 weeks, then a deload - linear progression, rep ranges vary by day and lift role, and RIR is calibrated to your experience.

  Weekly per-muscle set plan (freq/wk · week-1 sets → peak-week sets · MEV–MRV landmark):
     abs          3×/wk   6→9      sets   (MEV 6 · MRV 25)
     back         3×/wk   10→12    sets   (MEV 10 · MRV 25)
     biceps       3×/wk   8→9      sets   (MEV 8 · MRV 20)
     chest        3×/wk   10→12    sets   (MEV 10 · MRV 22)
     glutes       3×/wk   8→12     sets   (MEV 4 · MRV 16)
     hamstrings   3×/wk   8→12     sets   (MEV 6 · MRV 20)
     quads        3×/wk   8→12     sets   (MEV 8 · MRV 20)
     shoulders    3×/wk   8→12     sets   (MEV 8 · MRV 22)
     triceps      3×/wk   8→9      sets   (MEV 8 · MRV 18)

  RULE TRACE (why these numbers):
     • Experience "novice" (~2yr training age): base 10 sets/muscle/wk, +1/wk ramp, ≤6 sets/muscle/session, periodization="linear".
     • Goal "general_fitness": volume ×0.8, RIR band 2–3; rep zones primary 5–10 @ 75%1RM, accessory 10–15 @ 65%1RM.
     • RIR floors (never trained closer than): compound 2, isolation 2. Knob shift +0. Novice self-report calibration +1.

     • Session-length recipe (30 min): "Full Body B" trimmed 9→5 exercises (kept the highest-priority lifts).
     • Session-length recipe (30 min): "Full Body C" trimmed 9→5 exercises (kept the highest-priority lifts).

     • Experience "intermediate" (~4yr training age): base 13 sets/muscle/wk, +1.5/wk ramp, ≤8 sets/muscle/session, periodization="dup".
     • Goal "team_sport": volume ×0.85, RIR band 2–3; rep zones primary 3–6 @ 82%1RM, accessory 8–12 @ 68%1RM.
     • RIR floors (never trained closer than): compound 1, isolation 1. Knob shift +0. Novice self-report calibration +0.
     • Deload cadence: every 5 weeks. Progression load ×1.
     • In-season team sport: capped to 1–2 short maintenance sessions to spare the legs for matches (Rønnestad 2011).
     • Split for 3 day(s): Power + Plyometrics · Full-Body Strength.
     • Injury swap in "Power + Plyometrics": Seated Box Jump for the quads slot (safer choice for knees).
     • Injury swap in "Full-Body Strength": Step-Up for the quads slot (safer choice for knees).
     • Injury swap in "Full-Body Strength": Bodyweight Squat for the glutes slot (safer choice for knees).
     • Game day = Sat: strength placed at MD-3 (far from match, CNS-loading), priming at MD-1; conditioning kept ≥6h from heavy legs (concurrent-training guard, RESEARCH.md §7.4).
     • In-season phase: strength held at maintenance (lower volume, higher relative intensity, 3–6 reps); leg volume kept low to preserve match readiness.

================================================================================================
CONTRAST — how EXPERIENCE and the CONFIG KNOBS change the output
================================================================================================

A) Same goal (hypertrophy, 4d/wk, chest priority), 3 experience levels — week-1 chest volume + primary RIR:
   beginner      chest 2×/wk 11→15 sets/wk · deload every 6wk · a primary set ran at RIR 4 (RPE 6)
   intermediate  chest 2×/wk 16→22 sets/wk · deload every 6wk · a primary set ran at RIR 3 (RPE 7)
   advanced      chest 2×/wk 20→22 sets/wk · deload every 6wk · a primary set ran at RIR 3 (RPE 7)

B) Same lifter (intermediate hypertrophy), failure-proximity knob cautious→balanced→aggressive — week-1 primary RIR:
   cautious    → a primary set: RIR 4 (RPE 6)   [floor enforced by experience+knob]
   balanced    → a primary set: RIR 3 (RPE 7)   [floor enforced by experience+knob]
   aggressive  → a primary set: RIR 2 (RPE 8)   [floor enforced by experience+knob]

C) Safety clamp — a NOVICE on "aggressive" failure-proximity is still held to the experience RIR floor:
   novice + aggressive → primary compound RIR 3 (never below 2 for a novice on compounds, whatever the knob says).

D) Deload-frequency knob (intermediate) infrequent→standard→frequent — accumulation length before a deload week:
   infrequent  → 7 accumulation weeks, then deload (week 8).
   standard    → 5 accumulation weeks, then deload (week 6).
   frequent    → 3 accumulation weeks, then deload (week 4).

END OF SAMPLE RUN — all six profiles generated with zero errors.
```
